title: Composite academic honor-code baseline
text: A student violates the honor code when submitting work that is not their own, or that incorporates substantial unattributed text or ideas of another, or that misrepresents the origin of cited material. Violations include: plagiarism (verbatim/near-verbatim use without attribution), self-plagiarism (resubmission across courses), collusion (unauthorized collaboration), fabrication (citing non-existent sources or data), AI-misuse where prohibited by syllabus.

title: Plagiarism taxonomy
text: 1. Verbatim plagiarism: direct copying. 2. Mosaic plagiarism: paragraphs reassembled from multiple sources without attribution. 3. Paraphrase plagiarism: source language reordered but ideas unattributed. 4. Self-plagiarism: prior work resubmitted as new. 5. Citation laundering: paraphrasing one source while citing a different (often unread) source.

title: Default AI use policy (composite)
text: AI assistants may be used for: brainstorming, grammar/spell checking, retrieval of well-known facts. AI assistants may NOT be used for: generating substantive prose presented as the student's own, generating citations (high fabrication risk), completing graded assessments unless explicitly permitted by the syllabus. Students must disclose AI use in a declaration appended to each submission.

title: APA 7 in-text citation format
text: In-text: (Author, Year) for paraphrase; (Author, Year, p. NN) for direct quote. Reference list entries follow the format: Author, A. A. (Year). Title of work. Publisher. https://doi.org/xxxx. Three or more authors: first author + et al.

title: MLA 9 citation format
text: In-text: (Author Page). Works Cited: Author. Title. Container, contributors, version, number, publisher, publication date, location. URL or DOI for online sources.

title: Severity → recommended sanction (composite)
text: Minor (first offense, ≤10% of submission): warning + redo. Moderate (verbatim block, 10-30% of submission OR fabricated citation): F-on-assignment + honor-code education. Major (substantial unattributed work, AI-misuse on graded assessment, repeat offense): F-in-course + transcript notation + Honor Committee review. Severe (collusion ring, falsified data, multiple repeat offenses): suspension + transcript notation + possible expulsion.

title: AI detector caveats
text: All AI detectors have non-zero false-positive rates, especially on non-native-English writing. NEVER conclude AI-misuse from a single detector with confidence > 0.9. Require: (a) two independent detectors agreeing, OR (b) linguistic burstiness analysis showing GPT-typical perplexity flatness, OR (c) explicit AI-artefact phrases ('as an AI language model', 'my knowledge cutoff is...'). Allow the student to demonstrate their writing process (drafts, version history).
